title: Agent Systems Dataset Schema
description: Canonical field definitions for qualifying agent systems. A qualifying system must pass all four rstack criteria: persona, file access, remote accessibility, and async scheduling.

system.description: Top-level identity fields for the agent system.

id.description: Slug identifier for the system, lowercase, hyphenated. E.g. 'openclaw', 'claude-dispatch'.

name.description: Display name of the agent system.

vendor.description: Organization or individual that builds and maintains the system.

version.description: Version evaluated. Semver or date-based. 'latest' if pinned to current.

primary_language.description: Primary programming language or runtime for building with the system.

license.description: License of the core system. 'proprietary' for closed-source.

as_of.description: Date this record was last verified. ISO 8601.

qualification.description: The four rstack qualifying criteria. All must be true for a system to be included in this dataset.

qualifies.description: True if the system passes all four criteria.

persona.description: Can the system take on a consistent identity or character?

passes.description: True if the system supports persona/identity configuration.

system_prompt_support.description: Can a custom system prompt or instructions be set per agent?

character_persistence.description: How identity/character is persisted across invocations. 'session' means within a single run only; 'file' means via a config/character file; 'database' means via persistent storage.

multi_persona_support.description: Can multiple distinct personas/agents with different identities be run from the same system?

notes.description: Free-text notes on persona implementation details.

file_access.description: Can the system read and write files on an always-on computer?

passes.description: True if the system supports file read/write on a host computer.

scope.description: 'full' = arbitrary OS paths; 'sandboxed' = restricted execution environment; 'container' = Docker/VM-scoped; 'none' = no file access.

read.description: Can the agent read files from the filesystem?

write.description: Can the agent write or modify files on the filesystem?

os_support.description: Operating systems on which file access is supported.

built_in_tools.description: Named built-in tools or primitives for file access, if any. E.g. ['FileReadTool', 'FileWriterTool'].

notes.description: Free-text notes on file access implementation.

remote_accessibility.description: Can the system be reached and directed remotely?

passes.description: True if the system can be controlled from a remote client.

managed_cloud.description: Is there a managed cloud offering that hosts the agent and provides a stable endpoint?

self_hosted_api.description: Can the operator self-host the agent and expose it via an API?

mobile_app.description: Is there a first-party mobile app for remote control?

async_scheduling.description: Can the system schedule and execute work asynchronously without a human in the loop?

passes.description: True if at least one native scheduling primitive is built in.

cron.description: Native cron or interval-based scheduling built into the system (not requiring external tools).

event_driven.description: Can the system react to events (messages, state changes) and execute work asynchronously?

webhook.description: Can the system receive and respond to incoming webhook calls?

manual_trigger.description: Can a human or script trigger a run on demand without being in-line with the execution?

external_scheduler_required.description: True if cron/scheduling requires an external tool (e.g. APScheduler, Celery, cloud cron). A system can pass the criterion without this being false if event_driven or webhook alone suffice.

comparison.description: Commonly published dimensions for comparing agent systems.

cost_model.description: How operators and users pay to use the system.

pricing_type.description: One or more pricing models that apply.

free_tier.description: Is there a free tier or free self-hosted option?

cloud_pricing_url.description: URL to the official pricing page for the managed/cloud offering, if applicable.

notes.description: Free-text notes on pricing, including typical cost ranges if known.

context_window.description: Context window available to the agent during a single run.

max_tokens.description: Maximum context window in tokens. Null if not applicable or variable by underlying model.

determined_by.description: Whether the context limit is set by the framework itself or inherited from the underlying model.

notes.description: Free-text notes, e.g. if context size varies by model choice.

tool_types.description: Categories of tools the agent can use.

web_browsing.description: Can the agent browse the web or fetch URLs?

code_execution.description: Can the agent execute code (Python, JS, shell, etc.)?

file_operations.description: Can the agent read/write files via a tool interface?

browser_automation.description: Can the agent automate a real browser (e.g. Playwright, Selenium)?

custom_tools.description: Can operators define and register arbitrary custom tools or function calls?

mcp_support.description: Does the system support the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool integration?

memory_types.description: Memory mechanisms available to the agent.

in_context.description: Conversation/session history passed in the context window.

external_storage.description: Agent can read/write to an external store (database, vector store, file) between runs.

persistent_memory.description: First-class persistent memory that survives across sessions without manual wiring.

vector_store.description: Built-in or natively integrated vector store for semantic memory/retrieval.

underlying_models.description: Which AI models the system can use.

model_agnostic.description: Can the operator choose from multiple model providers?

default_models.description: Default or primary models the system is built around.

supported_providers.description: Model providers whose models can be used with this system.

data_source.description: How this record's data was obtained.

confidence.description: Confidence level in the accuracy of this record. 'verified' = confirmed against live official docs.

source_urls.description: URLs of official documentation used to verify this record.

last_verified.description: Date the record was last checked against live sources.

needs_reverification.description: Flag set when a field is known to change frequently or was not live-verified.
